verses.latin_vulgate: Qui regis Israël, intende ;  qui deducis velut ovem Joseph.  Qui sedes super cherubim, manifestare
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רֹ֘עֵ֤ה יִשְׂרָאֵ֨ל ׀ הַאֲזִ֗ינָה נֹהֵ֣ג כַּצֹּ֣אן יוֹסֵ֑ף יֹשֵׁ֖ב הַכְּרוּבִ֣ים הוֹפִֽיעָה׃
verses.king_james: Give ear, O Shepherd of Israel, thou that leadest Joseph like a flock; thou that dwellest between the cherubims, shine forth.

verses.latin_vulgate: coram Ephraim, Benjamin, et Manasse.  Excita potentiam tuam, et veni,  ut salvos facias nos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לִפְנֵ֤י אֶפְרַ֨יִם ׀ וּבִנְיָ֘מִ֤ן וּמְנַשֶּׁ֗ה עוֹרְרָ֥ה אֶת־גְּבוּרָתֶ֑ךָ וּלְכָ֖ה לִישֻׁעָ֣תָה לָּֽנוּ׃
verses.king_james: Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh stir up thy strength, and come and save us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus, converte nos, et ostende faciem tuam,  et salvi erimus.
verses.king_james: Turn us again, O God, and cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Domine Deus virtutum,  quousque irasceris super orationem servi tui ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהִ֣ים צְבָא֑וֹת עַד־מָתַ֥י עָ֝שַׁ֗נְתָּ בִּתְפִלַּ֥ת עַמֶּֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: O Lord God of hosts, how long wilt thou be angry against the prayer of thy people?

verses.latin_vulgate: cibabis nos pane lacrimarum,  et potum dabis nobis in lacrimis in mensura ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֶ֭אֱכַלְתָּם לֶ֣חֶם דִּמְעָ֑ה וַ֝תַּשְׁקֵ֗מוֹ בִּדְמָע֥וֹת שָׁלִֽישׁ׃
verses.king_james: Thou feedest them with the bread of tears; and givest them tears to drink in great measure.

verses.latin_vulgate: Posuisti nos in contradictionem vicinis nostris,  et inimici nostri subsannaverunt nos.
verses.king_james: Thou makest us a strife unto our neighbours: and our enemies laugh among themselves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus virtutum, converte nos, et ostende faciem tuam,  et salvi erimus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱלֹהִ֣ים צְבָא֣וֹת הֲשִׁיבֵ֑נוּ וְהָאֵ֥ר פָּ֝נֶ֗יךָ וְנִוָּשֵֽׁעָה׃
verses.king_james: Turn us again, O God of hosts, and cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vineam de Ægypto transtulisti :  ejecisti gentes, et plantasti eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גֶּ֭פֶן מִמִּצְרַ֣יִם תַּסִּ֑יעַ תְּגָרֵ֥שׁ גּ֝וֹיִ֗ם וַתִּטָּעֶֽהָ׃
verses.king_james: Thou hast brought a vine out of Egypt: thou hast cast out the heathen, and planted it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dux itineris fuisti in conspectu ejus ;  plantasti radices ejus, et implevit terram.
verses.king_james: Thou preparedst room before it, and didst cause it to take deep root, and it filled the land.

verses.latin_vulgate: Operuit montes umbra ejus,  et arbusta ejus cedros Dei.
verses.king_james: The hills were covered with the shadow of it, and the boughs thereof were like the goodly cedars.

verses.latin_vulgate: Extendit palmites suos usque ad mare,  et usque ad flumen propagines ejus.
verses.king_james: She sent out her boughs unto the sea, and her branches unto the river.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ut quid destruxisti maceriam ejus,  et vindemiant eam omnes qui prætergrediuntur viam ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָ֭מָּה פָּרַ֣צְתָּ גְדֵרֶ֑יהָ וְ֝אָר֗וּהָ כׇּל־עֹ֥בְרֵי דָֽרֶךְ׃
verses.king_james: Why hast thou then broken down her hedges, so that all they which pass by the way do pluck her?

verses.latin_vulgate: Exterminavit eam aper de silva,  et singularis ferus depastus est eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְכַרְסְמֶ֣נָּֽה חֲזִ֣יר מִיָּ֑ר וְזִ֖יז שָׂדַ֣י יִרְעֶֽנָּה׃
verses.king_james: The boar out of the wood doth waste it, and the wild beast of the field doth devour it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus virtutum, convertere,  respice de cælo, et vide,  et visita vineam istam :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱלֹהִ֣ים צְבָאוֹת֮ שֽׁ֫וּב־נָ֥א הַבֵּ֣ט מִשָּׁמַ֣יִם וּרְאֵ֑ה וּ֝פְקֹ֗ד גֶּ֣פֶן זֹֽאת׃
verses.king_james: Return, we beseech thee, O God of hosts: look down from heaven, and behold, and visit this vine;

verses.latin_vulgate: et perfice eam quam plantavit dextera tua,  et super filium hominis quem confirmasti tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְ֭כַנָּה אֲשֶׁר־נָטְעָ֣ה יְמִינֶ֑ךָ וְעַל־בֵּ֝֗ן אִמַּ֥צְתָּה לָּֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: And the vineyard which thy right hand hath planted, and the branch that thou madest strong for thyself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Incensa igni et suffossa,  ab increpatione vultus tui peribunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׂרֻפָ֣ה בָאֵ֣שׁ כְּסוּחָ֑ה מִגַּעֲרַ֖ת פָּנֶ֣יךָ יֹאבֵֽדוּ׃
verses.king_james: It is burned with fire, it is cut down: they perish at the rebuke of thy countenance.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fiat manus tua super virum dexteræ tuæ,  et super filium hominis quem confirmasti tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תְּֽהִי־יָ֭דְךָ עַל־אִ֣ישׁ יְמִינֶ֑ךָ עַל־בֶּן־אָ֝דָ֗ם אִמַּ֥צְתָּ לָּֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: Let thy hand be upon the man of thy right hand, upon the son of man whom thou madest strong for thyself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non discedimus a te :  vivificabis nos, et nomen tuum invocabimus.
verses.king_james: So will not we go back from thee: quicken us, and we will call upon thy name.

verses.latin_vulgate: Domine Deus virtutum, converte nos, et ostende faciem tuam,  et salvi erimus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְ֘הֹוָ֤ה אֱלֹהִ֣ים צְבָא֣וֹת הֲשִׁיבֵ֑נוּ הָאֵ֥ר פָּ֝נֶ֗יךָ וְנִוָּשֵֽׁעָה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Turn us again, O Lord God of hosts, cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.
